title: **Keycloak claim mapping coordination**: ACMP requires specific group/realm-role claim names from Keycloak. If the org's Keycloak admin cannot expose the required claims in the required format before
description: **Keycloak claim mapping coordination**: ACMP requires specific group/realm-role claim names from Keycloak. If the org's Keycloak admin cannot expose the required claims in the required format before PH-1 build starts, identity integration is blocked. (Category: Technical / External dependency; Exposure: High)
mitigation: **Mitigated/Closed (ADR-0015):** ACMP now self-hosts Keycloak with an ACMP-owned realm, so ACMP defines the claim names itself — there is no external Keycloak admin to coordinate with and no cross-org claim-mapping dependency. (Historical fallback: static role config for dev/staging.)

If the org's Keycloak admin cannot expose the required claims in the required format before PH-1 build starts, identity integration is blocked. (Category: Technical / External dependency; Exposure: High)", "Impact": "H", "Likelihood": "M", "Mitigation": "**Mitigated/Closed (ADR-0015):** ACMP now self-hosts Keycloak with an ACMP-owned realm, so ACMP defines the claim names itself — there is no external Keycloak admin to coordinate with and no cross-org claim-mapping dependency. (Historical fallback: static role config for dev/staging.)", "Owner": "Tech Lead", "MVP/Full": "MVP", "Status": "Closed"}}

title: **Bilingual/RTL effort underestimated**: RTL layout with logical CSS requires discipline across all UI components; a single LTR layout smuggled in silently creates a regression. EN↔AR locale switching
description: **Bilingual/RTL effort underestimated**: RTL layout with logical CSS requires discipline across all UI components; a single LTR layout smuggled in silently creates a regression. EN↔AR locale switching with unsaved-data protection adds surface area. Total RTL effort is consistently underestimated in similar projects. (Category: Technical; Exposure: High)
mitigation: Bilingual and RTL are first-class (P-07) from day 1, not a retrofit. All React components use logical CSS properties. Automated RTL visual regression test (Playwright, both languages) runs in CI on every PR. Dedicated RTL pass in Definition of Done (docs/execution/definition-of-done.md).

All React components use logical CSS properties. Automated RTL visual regression test (Playwright, both languages) runs in CI on every PR. Dedicated RTL pass in Definition of Done (docs/execution/definition-of-done.md).", "Owner": "Tech Lead / Frontend", "MVP/Full": "MVP", "Status": "Open"}}

title: **Colluding privileged insiders**: A Chairman + Secretary acting together could attempt to alter or suppress a vote or decision record. Collusion cannot be prevented by software alone. (Category: Secu
description: **Colluding privileged insiders**: A Chairman + Secretary acting together could attempt to alter or suppress a vote or decision record. Collusion cannot be prevented by software alone. (Category: Security / Governance; Exposure: Medium)
mitigation: Immutability enforced at code level (no UPDATE/DELETE on audit rows). SHA-256 hash chain over vote + issued-decision audit records makes undetected tampering computationally infeasible (ADR-0009). Auditor role can independently verify chain integrity. Human/process controls (independent audit, legal) are the remaining layer. Accept residual risk.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "RISK-003", "Risk": "**Colluding privileged insiders**: A Chairman + Secretary acting together could attempt to alter or suppress a vote or decision record. Collusion cannot be prevented by software alone. SHA-256 hash chain over vote + issued-decision audit records makes undetected tampering computationally infeasible (ADR-0009). Auditor role can independently verify chain integrity. Human/process controls (independent audit, legal) are the remaining layer. Accept residual risk.", "Owner": "Security Reviewer", "MVP/Full": "MVP", "Status": "Monitoring"}}

title: **At-rest encryption keys stored on-host (no HSM)**: At this scale (on-prem VM, ≤20 users, no budget for HSM), database and MinIO encryption keys will be stored on the same host. This is a weaker key-
description: **At-rest encryption keys stored on-host (no HSM)**: At this scale (on-prem VM, ≤20 users, no budget for HSM), database and MinIO encryption keys will be stored on the same host. This is a weaker key-protection posture than HSM. (Category: Security; Exposure: Low)
mitigation: Risk is proportional to scale and classification. Mitigations: full-disk encryption on VM; keys in secrets manager (env vars / Docker secrets, not in image); access control at VM/OS layer; document risk explicitly. Accept residual risk as proportional to org's risk appetite for an internal tool. Re-evaluate if data classification escalates.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "RISK-004", "Risk": "**At-rest encryption keys stored on-host (no HSM)**: At this scale (on-prem VM, ≤20 users, no budget for HSM), database and MinIO encryption keys will be stored on the same host. This is a weaker key-protection posture than HSM. (Category: Security; Exposure: Low)", "Impact": "M", "Likelihood": "L", "Mitigation": "Risk is proportional to scale and classification. Mitigations: full-disk encryption on VM; keys in secrets manager (env vars / Docker secrets, not in image); access control at VM/OS layer; document risk explicitly. Accept residual risk as proportional to org's risk appetite for an internal tool. Re-evaluate if data classification escalates.", "Owner": "Security Reviewer", "MVP/Full": "MVP", "Status": "Accepted"}}

title: **Tarseem early maturity (v1.0.0, ~9 days old at PH-0)**: Released 2026-06-17. Schema declared frozen at v1.0, but as a new project it may have undiscovered bugs, missing capabilities, or schema corre
description: **Tarseem early maturity (v1.0.0, ~9 days old at PH-0)**: Released 2026-06-17. Schema declared frozen at v1.0, but as a new project it may have undiscovered bugs, missing capabilities, or schema corrections in early patches. (Category: Technical / External dependency; Exposure: Medium)
mitigation: JSON spec is the version-controlled source of truth (ADR-0006). Render artifacts are re-generatable; a broken render is never blocking. ACMP pins to a specific Tarseem release tag in the sidecar Dockerfile. Monitor Tarseem releases; upgrade only after review. Maintain a fallback for diagram-type gaps (attach raw spec + manual export).
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "RISK-005", "Risk": "**Tarseem early maturity (v1.0.0, ~9 days old at PH-0)**: Released 2026-06-17. title: **Scope creep toward generic PM**: Stakeholders may request sprint boards, team capacity planning, resource allocation, or other generic PM features that are explicitly out of scope (OOS-01). (Categor
description: **Scope creep toward generic PM**: Stakeholders may request sprint boards, team capacity planning, resource allocation, or other generic PM features that are explicitly out of scope (OOS-01). (Category: Product / Governance; Exposure: Medium)
mitigation: Reference P-01 ("architecture governance, not PM") in every sprint review. Any feature request that is not traceable to an FR or a new accepted OQ is rejected. Out-of-scope exclusions (OOS-01–OOS-15) are cited by ID.

title: **Committee adoption risk**: Members revert to the text-file process if the platform adds friction or lacks critical features. Adoption failure would make PH-1 delivery irrelevant. (Category: Product
description: **Committee adoption risk**: Members revert to the text-file process if the platform adds friction or lacks critical features. Adoption failure would make PH-1 delivery irrelevant. (Category: Product / Change management; Exposure: High)
mitigation: Involve Secretary and ≥1 champion member in PH-1 design review. Conduct a pilot run of one real committee meeting on the platform before go-live. Gather feedback; address Sev-1 UX blockers before PH-1 go-live. Maintain parallel text-file as fallback only during transition; communicate end-date.

title: **Webex licensing / Webex Assistant enablement**: Webex transcript retrieval (FR-058) requires Webex Assistant to be enabled — this cannot be done programmatically and requires host/Control Hub config
description: **Webex licensing / Webex Assistant enablement**: Webex transcript retrieval (FR-058) requires Webex Assistant to be enabled — this cannot be done programmatically and requires host/Control Hub configuration or policy. Licensing for bot/integration accounts may require procurement. (Category: External dependency; Exposure: High)
mitigation: Webex features are Phase 2 (ADR-0005); in-app notifications operate in PH-1 without Webex. INotificationChannel abstraction means Webex is purely additive. Initiate Webex licensing conversation in PH-0 so it doesn't block PH-2. Treat transcript unavailability as an accepted PH-2 fallback.
custom_attributes: {"v1": {"ID": "RISK-008", "Risk": "**Webex licensing / Webex Assistant enablement**: Webex transcript retrieval (FR-058) requires Webex Assistant to be enabled — this cannot be done programmatically and requires host/Control Hub configuration or policy. Licensing for bot/integration accounts may require procurement. (Category: External dependency; Exposure: High)", "Impact": "M", "Likelihood": "H", "Mitigation": "Webex features are Phase 2 (ADR-0005); in-app notifications operate in PH-1 without Webex. INotificationChannel abstraction means Webex is purely additive. Initiate Webex licensing conversation in PH-0 so it doesn't block PH-2. title: **Air-gapped / private-registry dependency and image mirroring**: On-prem deployment may require all container images (ACMP app, SQL Server, Seq, MinIO, Tarseem, Node/Chromium base images) to be mirro
description: **Air-gapped / private-registry dependency and image mirroring**: On-prem deployment may require all container images (ACMP app, SQL Server, Seq, MinIO, Tarseem, Node/Chromium base images) to be mirrored to a private registry. NuGet and npm packages may need internal mirrors. If mirror setup is not coordinated early, deployment is blocked. (Category: Infrastructure / External; Exposure: High)
mitigation: OQ-REGISTRY and OQ-NUGET-MIRROR items in docs/decisions/open-decision-register.md. Default recommendation: use public registries in dev/staging; document all image references with digests for prod pull. Coordinate with org's IT/infrastructure team for private registry setup as a PH-0/PH-1 delivery dependency.

title: **Arabic FTS word-breaker quality in SQL Server**: SQL Server's built-in Arabic word-breaker may produce poor-quality search results for committee-specific terminology, especially technical terms tran
description: **Arabic FTS word-breaker quality in SQL Server**: SQL Server's built-in Arabic word-breaker may produce poor-quality search results for committee-specific terminology, especially technical terms transliterated from English. Users searching in AR may get poor recall/precision. (Category: Technical; Exposure: Medium)
mitigation: PH-0 spike: run 20 sample Arabic committee queries through SQL Server FTS and score results. If quality is insufficient, escalate OQ-AR-FTS to consider Meilisearch (self-hosted, app-owned) as a Phase 2 search backend behind the same abstraction. Do not commit to Meilisearch until spike results are known.

title: **AI extraction privacy (Phase 3)**: Transcript content sent to an LLM endpoint may contain sensitive committee deliberations. If the LLM endpoint is cloud-based, data may leave the on-prem environmen
description: **AI extraction privacy (Phase 3)**: Transcript content sent to an LLM endpoint may contain sensitive committee deliberations. If the LLM endpoint is cloud-based, data may leave the on-prem environment. OWASP LLM01 (prompt injection) applies to AI-extracted candidates. (Category: Security / Privacy; Exposure: Critical)
mitigation: AI extraction is Phase 3 (settled decision); manual first. Feature is OFF by default; Admin activation requires explicit data-residency confirmation logged in the system. LLM endpoint must be on-prem or org-approved cloud with data-processing agreement. Human approval gate mandatory — no AI content enters records without Secretary approval. Candidate content marked as provisional.

title: 4 GiB is tight; a Keycloak GC burst plus SQL Server's buffer pool can OOM the t3.medium
description: The consolidated stack budgets ~3.95 GiB across 6 services on a 4 GiB host. SQL Server's buffer pool and a Keycloak GC spike can collide. (Category: Operational/Resource; Exposure: High)
mitigation: Per-service memory limits retuned for a 2 vCPU host (the current overlay's cpus:4 is meaningless on t3.medium); 4 GiB swapfile; Keycloak -Xmx384m; drop Seq from UAT. AC-084 asserts no OOM-kill in docker events during an e2e run. Escalate to t3.large if it recurs — upsize, do not split (splitting is 1.8-2x cost and breaks single-host Compose per CON-003).

title: T3 launches in 'unlimited' credit mode by default — sustained CPU above baseline bills a silent surcharge
description: AWS docs: T3/T3a/T4g launch as unlimited by default; CPU above the 20% baseline (t3.medium) incurs a per-vCPU-hour surcharge with no cap. (Category: Cost; Exposure: Medium)
mitigation: Launch with --credit-specification CpuCredits=standard: a hard performance cap instead of an unbounded bill. CloudWatch alarm on CPUCreditBalance. T3 credits survive 7 days across a stop, which suits on-demand UAT.

title: 30 GB EBS is insufficient once uncompressed backups, two DBs and Docker images are accounted for
description: The root volume holds AL2023, a 4 GiB swapfile, ~6 GB of images (sqlserver-fts alone ~1.6-2 GB), /var/opt/mssql (two DBs up to 10 GB each on Express), 30 days of now-uncompressed .bak files, Seq data and logs. (Category: Operational/Resource; Exposure: Medium)
mitigation: Provision 50 GB gp3 ($4/mo); cut on-box backup retention to 7 days with older copies in S3; CloudWatch disk-usage alarm at 75%.

title: No rollback path — EF migrations are forward-only and run before the app starts
description: db-migrate runs migrations before api/worker; migrations are forward-only. An older image digest against an already-migrated schema is undefined behavior. A failed Keycloak Liquibase run additionally holds DATABASECHANGELOGLOCK, wedging every later KC start. (Category: Operational/DR; Exposure: High)
mitigation: Slice P23 ships a DR runbook: mandatory pre-deploy .bak, documented restore-to-point, an explicit roll-forward-only clause in ADR-0034, and a DATABASECHANGELOGLOCK unwedge procedure.

title: The Keycloak admin console and master-realm token endpoint become internet-facing under /kc/
description: default.conf.template proxies all of /kc/, including /kc/admin and /kc/realms/master. On a real domain this is a password-brute-force surface (was semi-obscure behind ngrok). (Category: Security; Exposure: High)
mitigation: nginx deny on /kc/admin and /kc/realms/master from public; admin access via SSH/SSM tunnel only — consistent with the existing 'admin consoles bind 127.0.0.1' posture (slice P25).

title: A shared instance role lets a compromised UAT box hijack production DNS
description: One instance role with route53:ChangeResourceRecordSets over the zone would let a compromised UAT host UPSERT acmp.anas7ammo.dev and redirect production traffic. Contradicts AC-083 isolation. (Category: Security; Exposure: High)
mitigation: One IAM role per environment, scoped with route53:ChangeResourceRecordSetsNormalizedRecordNames to that host's own record only (slice P20).

title: The >=95% per-file coverage gate breaks on the new S3 denied-create branch
description: coverlet.runsettings states MinioFileStore is NO LONGER excluded; ci.yml enforces >=95% lines per file. The new denied-create tolerance branch in EnsureBucketAsync adds uncovered lines to a 60-line file. (Category: Quality/CI; Exposure: Medium)
mitigation: Slice P22 budgets test work for the denied-create branch using the existing MinioFileStoreTests Testcontainers harness; check-coverage.mjs must stay green before merge.

title: UAT/prod edition drift — Express-only failures first surface in production
description: If UAT runs Developer and prod runs Express, Express-only failure modes (backup compression, FTS-under-Express, 1410 MB buffer pool, 10 GB cap) first appear in production, defeating the point of staging. (Category: Quality/Release; Exposure: High)
mitigation: Run SQL Server Express in BOTH environments (ADR-0037). Non-negotiable.

title: A long-lived IAM access key lives on-box (operator's chosen S3 auth)
description: The operator chose to reuse the Minio SDK with a static IAM user key rather than an instance role, so a long-lived credential sits in a Docker secret on the host indefinitely. (Category: Security; Exposure: Medium)
mitigation: Bucket-scoped IAM policy; Docker secret (ADR-0032); scheduled rotation. Documented upgrade path: AWSSDK.S3 + EC2 instance role removes the static key entirely (ADR-0035).

title: No free-tier cushion — every hour bills and a misconfiguration bills silently
description: The AWS account's 12-month free tier expired 2025-11-02, so there is no allowance to absorb mistakes; a runaway resource bills in real dollars. (Category: Cost; Exposure: Medium)
mitigation: AWS Budgets alerts (50/80%), a deny-IAM action at 100% and auto-stop of Project=ACMP instances at 120%; Cost Anomaly Detection; UAT stopped by default. Caveat: budget data lags 8-12h and cannot restrain root, which is why P20 moves off root first.

title: A previously org-network-only app becomes internet-facing
description: On-prem ACMP was reachable only inside the org network; the cloud deployment exposes it publicly, widening the attack surface. (Category: Security; Exposure: Medium)
mitigation: Security group allows only 443 + SSH-from-IP (or SSM, no SSH port); no port 80 (DNS-01 TLS); the P16 ASVS L2 controls already shipped. Consider SSM Session Manager to remove port 22 entirely.

title: Accepted residuals: Express 10 GB/DB cap, single-AZ no-HA, and root-credential operation
description: Three residual exposures accepted under existing constraints: SQL Express caps each database at 10 GB as audit/Hangfire history grows; a single instance in a single AZ has no HA; operating as the AWS root user defeats IAM guardrails including the budget auto-stop action. (Category: Mixed; Exposure: Medium)
mitigation: Monitor sys.database_files + Hangfire retention (10 GB cap); accept single-AZ under CON-004 with RTO<=8h via restore; move off root to an IAM admin + MFA in slice P20 (a prerequisite for the budget action to bind).
